title: Hieracium tabergense
intro: Hieracium tabergense is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly found in mountainous regions and characterized by its yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. Traditionally, Hieracium tabergense has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium tabergense is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Hieracium tabergense is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes hawkweeds, and is native to the mountainous regions of northern Europe. The species is classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium tabergense shares similarities with other dandelion-like plants. This classification also suggests a close relationship with other members of the Hieracium genus.
